pi_items.source: task.json description and setup_task.sh line 27, export_result.sh line 49
task.json description states '500 ml (± 5 ml)', confirmed in verifier.py line 23: TARGET_VOLUME = 500.0
task.json description states '± 5 ml', confirmed in verifier.py line 24: TOLERANCE = 5.0, and export_result.sh
task.json description states 'height of exactly 15 cm' and 'x-axis from 0 to 15'

No mention of height_tolerance in task.json description. Verifier.py does not check height in any form (lines 26-119 only check: file creation, function definition, surface command, integral command, volume accuracy). Task description states height should be 'exactly 15 cm' with no tolerance mentioned.

Task description line 5 states this. Web sources confirm: 1 milliliter is defined as exactly 1 cubic centimeter by SI convention. See https://www.inchcalculator.com/convert/milliliter-to-cubic-centimeter/ and https://www.hackmath.net/en/calculator/conversion-of-volume-units

Task description specifies the disk method integral. Web sources confirm this is the correct formula for volume of revolution around the x-axis. See https://math.libretexts.org/Courses/Cosumnes_River_College/Math_401:_Calculus_II_-_Integral_Calculus/01:_Applications_of_Integration/1.03:_Volumes_of_Revolution_-_The_Disk_and_Washer_Methods

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task where agents design a parametric bottle shape in GeoGebra. Key verified facts: (1) The expected output file is 'bottle_design.ggb' saved to ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/. (2) The target volume is 500 ml with a tolerance of ±5 ml (acceptable range: 495-505 ml). (3) The target height is 15 cm (x-axis domain 0 to 15). (4) The unit conversion 1 ml = 1 cm³ is mathematically correct. (5) The volume calculation uses the disk method formula V = π∫[0,15] r(x)² dx, which is the correct mathematical approach for volumes of revolution. (6) The verifier checks for five criteria worth 100 points total: file creation (10 pts), profile function (20 pts), Surface command (20 pts), Integral command (20 pts), and volume accuracy of 495-505 ml (30 pts), with a pass threshold of 70 points. One metadata claim (height_tolerance: 0.1) is unverified: it is not mentioned in the task description and the verifier does not check height in any form.
